name: Multicomponent Working Memory

source.author: Alan Baddeley
source.paper: Working memory: looking back and looking forward

biological_basis: Working memory is a limited-capacity system with a central executive coordinating phonological and visuospatial slave systems. It holds information actively for manipulation during cognitive tasks.
core_claim: Immediate conscious processing requires a small, actively maintained buffer (~4 items reliably). Capacity limits force selective attention and chunking strategies.

implementable_mechanism.description: Fixed-capacity slot buffer holding active representations. Each slot has content, activation level, and recency. Central executive routes information in/out and coordinates with long-term stores.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: if len(buffer) >= capacity: evict(least_active_or_oldest); buffer.insert(item, activation); return broadcast(buffer)

notes: Directly maps to WorkspaceBuffer with 7-slot default capacity.

name: Global Workspace Broadcasting
biological_basis: Conscious cognition arises when information wins a competition among specialised unconscious processors and is broadcast globally across the brain via a shared workspace, making it available to all modules.
core_claim: Conscious access = global broadcasting. Unconscious processors work in parallel; only one coalition at a time gains workspace access and becomes globally readable.

implementable_mechanism.description: Competition among input signals for limited workspace slots. Winners are broadcast to all downstream modules (prediction, reasoning, memory). Losers remain local.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: winners = select_top_k(signals, capacity, by=salience); workspace.set(winners); for module in modules: module.receive(workspace.broadcast())

notes: Core architectural principle: WorkspaceBuffer.broadcast() makes contents globally available.

name: Neuronal Global Workspace

source.author: Stanislas Dehaene, Michel Kerszberg, Jean-Pierre Changeux
source.paper: A neuronal model of a global workspace in effortful cognitive tasks
source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

biological_basis: A network of long-range excitatory neurons with non-linear ignition dynamics creates a global workspace. Sustained activity requires recurrent amplification; only strong enough coalitions ignite.
core_claim: Workspace access requires crossing an ignition threshold — weak signals decay, strong coalitions self-amplify and persist, enabling effortful cognition.

implementable_mechanism.description: Signals below salience threshold decay without entering workspace. Above threshold, signals enter slots with recurrent reinforcement (salience boost). Effortful tasks require sustained workspace occupancy.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: for signal in inputs: if signal.salience > threshold: workspace.ignite(signal); signal.salience += recurrent_boost; else: signal.decay()

notes: Informs AttentionGate salience threshold (0.3) before workspace insertion in EIDOS.
